symptoms.symptom_name: Prodromal localised tingling/burning on the lip preceding the eruption

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A localised tingling/burning prodrome a day before vesicles appear is characteristic of HSV reactivation and is the ideal window to start an antiviral.
Bacterial impetigo has no neural tingling prodrome; a sensory prodrome points to a herpetic rather than bacterial process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Grouped tense vesicles on an erythematous base at the vermilion border, some ruptured and weeping

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clustered vesicles on an erythematous base at the vermilion border is the hallmark morphology and distribution of herpes labialis.
Aphthae occur on internal non-keratinised mucosa and do not begin as grouped vesicles; an external vermilion-border vesicular cluster excludes aphthous ulceration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent lip episodes triggered by stress / being run down

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recurrent, stereotyped, stress-precipitated eruption at the same lip site is typical of HSV reactivation from latency.
Zoster is typically a single dermatomal episode, not a recurrent same-site lip eruption.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vesicles rupturing to shallow, painful serous ulcers

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progression from tense vesicles to shallow weeping erosions that will crust reflects the natural evolution of a herpetic lesion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mildly tender submandibular lump felt by the patient (reactive lymphadenopathy)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tender regional lymphadenopathy is a non-specific reactive response to a localised viral lesion; patient-palpable but not a clinician-confirmed sign.

symptoms.symptom_name: No intraoral ulcers, no target lesions elsewhere, no new medication, no fever, no spreading hot induration

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of target lesions, mucosal/ocular involvement and a new drug trigger argues strongly against SJS/EM.
No spreading hot induration and no fever argues against an expanding bacterial soft-tissue infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dermatological examination: a 1.5 cm cluster of grouped tense vesicles on a bright erythematous base at the vermilion border of the right lower lip, some ruptured; surrounding skin not indurated/hot; intraoral mucosa clear; tender right submandibular lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The precise morphology and vermilion-border distribution are characteristic — a clinician examination finding, sealed as it cannot be obtained via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: HSV-1 detection by PCR swab of a ruptured vesicle

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: PCR confirmation of HSV-1 is definitive but is unnecessary for management of a classic clinical presentation and is not available in real time via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: HSV PCR swab of a ruptured vesicle
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The clinical picture is characteristic of herpes labialis; PCR confirmation does not change acute management of a classic case and would over-investigate a benign presentation. Reserved for atypical, severe, or immunocompromised cases.
